responses.text: A woman in Japan committed suicide during isolation after she blamed herself for spreading COVID-19 to others.
The number of suicides in 2020 reached 20,919, the first increase since the financial crisis in 2009, with 6,976 women accounting for nearly 15% more than in 2019.
Lack of employment and unemployment and increasing the burden of caring for children and homes increase the amount of stress and nervous seizures in people during the coronavirus epidemic.
The number of suicides in Japan reached 21,077 in 2020, the first increase in 11 years, and even 2.5 times the number of deaths from COVID-19.
The problem of loneliness has become especially acute against the background of the pandemic.
The family environment in Japan has changed due to the coronavirus epidemic.
A long-term economic downturn caused by the epidemic could lead to a rebound in the suicide rate
Prime Minister of Japan Yoshihide Suga decided to introduce the post of Minister to combat loneliness and isolation.
About 40% of suicide prevention organizations in Japan have temporarily closed or reduced their working hours due to the epidemic
Many women in Japan are employed in casual or non-permanent jobs, making them more vulnerable to economic shocks.

A 30-year-old woman who was convalescing at home after being infected with the epidemic was found dead at her home, with a note saying she was worried about spreading the infection to people around her.
The number of suicides under the age of 20 was 659, an increase of 10.0% year-on-year, the highest since 2000.
Experts have warned that the epidemic could lead to a mental health crisis, mass unemployment and social isolation, and anxiety is affecting people around the world.
The suicide rate in Japan increased by 16% in July-October last year
The Japanese authorities intend to strengthen measures to combat the problem of loneliness
The problems of single people in Japan have worsened during the outbreak of the coronavirus.
The increase in suicide rates is linked to the second wave of the coronavirus pandemic
Some medical services have been suspended due to the epidemic.

Two people were injured when a Palestinian rocket fired in protest against the normalization of relations between the United Arab Emirates, Bahrain and Israel, and Israel responded by bombing the Gaza Strip.
Palestinian officials have described the agreements to normalize relations between the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain with Israel as a “betrayal of the Palestinian cause”.
Palestinian Deputy Prime Minister Ziad Abu Amr said that Palestine supports the creation of a multilateral international mechanism to resolve the conflict between Palestine and Israel.
Jordan opposes any plan that ignores Palestinian rights
Some Arab countries have turned their backs on the Palestinian issue

Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas said during his speech at a meeting of the secretaries general of Palestinian groups in Ramallah and Beirut that the last poisoned fighter that came to the Palestinian nation was the agreement between the United Arab Emirates and Israel with the support of the United States.

Palestinian President Mahmud Abbas has announced that he will no longer honor all agreements between the Palestinians and the United States and Israel as part of a protest against Israel ’ s occupation of parts of the West Bank.

Some Arab and Islamic countries, including the Palestinian Authority, Turkey, Iran and resistance groups, have taken a strong stance on the normalization of relations between the UAE and Israel and condemned this incident.
The Palestinian Authority president called on the Arab world to side with the Palestinian position that without Jerusalem there is no peace, no plan, no understanding and no negotiation.
The Palestinians are against terror and violence.
A compromise agreement with the Zionist regime cannot bring peace to Abu Dhabi and Manama
Palestinian Foreign Minister Riyad al-Maliki called on Arab League foreign ministers to condemn the agreement between the UAE and Israel to normalize relations.
Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas said "We will no longer accept that the United States is our only mediator in negotiations with Israel".
37.5% of foreign patients go to cosmetic surgery and dermatology hospitals
Hospitals in India, Singapore and South Korea receive more than 1 million foreign patients a year.

Currently, 12 hospitals and three limited surgical centers have permission to accept health tourists in Mashhad, and four hospitals and four other limited surgical centers are in the final stages of obtaining the relevant license.
Most health tourists in Mashhad are from Iraq, Afghanistan, Bahrain, Turkmenistan, Pakistan and Kuwait.
Popular destinations for dental tourism are Thailand, Vietnam, Mexico, Costa Rica, Hungary and Poland.

Iran has a high capacity in the fields of plastic surgery, minimally invasive and non-invasive cardiovascular treatment, complex heart surgery, infertility and specialized dental treatments, complex spine surgeries and modern laparoscopic treatment.
South Korea has one of the highest rates of plastic surgery in the world - about 1 million procedures per year, as well as the largest number of plastic surgeons per capita.
China's medical beauty market is growing faster than the world's.
Medical services in Thailand are 50% to 80% less than in the USA, Canada or Europe.
Plastic surgery in South Korea is most popular among residents of China, the United States, Australia, and Japan.
Chinese patients account for 31.2% of all foreign patients who come to Korea for medical treatment
A quarter of hospitals are active in health tourism and use their surplus capacity to provide services to foreign tourists.
Iran ranks 10th in terms of attracting health tourists
The most common procedures in dental tourism are the installation of implants, veneers and crowns, bleaching.
Thailand offers advanced medical services in the fields of orthopedics, oncology, plastic surgery, dentistry, ophthalmology, HIV and AIDS treatment and childbirth.
Nearly two-thirds of all medical malpractice cases were for cosmetic purposes.
The "Korean Wave" has led to a huge increase in the medical tourism industry in South Korea, especially the plastic surgery industry.
The widespread use of video conferencing services has caused a boom in demand for plastic surgery in the UK.
Treatment in Russia is cheaper, on dentistry foreigners can save up to 80% in dollar terms.
The place has been transformed into a park green space that people can use for walking, doing morning exercises, etc.
The park features creative environmental protection devices.
Urban forests have a significant cooling effect.
Neighborhood parks are a factor in improving city life.
The mayor’s office began to implement the project of small squares “Green Islands” as part of the city’s landscaping strategy.
Urbanization causes people to live in hotter, noisier, and more polluted environments.
Access to green spaces and natural environments is important for mental health, promotes social connections and physical activity, and reduces levels of stress, anxiety and depression.
The "Inclusive Park" is a space that provides people of all ages with the ability to rest and develop.
Pingshi Park has 3 lawns with flood stagnation function
Green plantations revitalize the air pool and improve the microclimate in the city.
The urban environment recreation project aims at creating spaces for citizens to spend their leisure time.
The municipality of District 4 designed and started the improvement and landscaping operations of the 3000 square meter space to beautify the urban landscape and promote social interactions.
About 70% of Taiwan ’ s residents live in urban areas affected by the heat island effect.
Just planting a trail tree can affect people ’ s mental health.

The Kapotnya district has been updated almost completely over the past year: on the embankment of the Moskva River, on the site of an empty territory, a large park appeared with a promenade, benches, picnic areas, observation platforms, several children's playgrounds, eco-trails, workout zones.
Pingshi Park provides leisure, art, and education functions in the surrounding area
The area has used the material of permeable asphalt as a demonstration to reduce the impact of heavy rain
Urban green space helps reduce air temperatures in urban heat island regions.
The project aims to improve the quality of life of residents
The city government launched the "Park 2.0" project for 10 parks located in the second and third stages of the walking city.
Urban life, crowding, overcrowding, traffic of vehicles, industrial equipment and ventilation devices play a major role in heating.
Green space can play a calming effect on the human immune system.
A children's playground will be built in the center of the "pocket square" on Enthusiasts street.
Garden furniture was installed in the park.
Turkey has contributed more than 150 TV series to an estimated 500 million viewers in 146 countries around the world.
Turkey uses “soft power” and focuses on religious, economic, and educational components to influence Georgia.
Turkey attracts talented children from ordinary families in Central Asia and other regions to its educational system.
Analysts believe that Muslims are mostly "shown negatively" in TV shows and movies, making the series a desirable change.
The impact of the series has attracted attention on social media.
The US $40 million series Kingdoms of Fire, jointly funded by the two governments, was broadcast in 2019, highlighting the Ottoman Empire as the tyrant who has ravaged the Arab world for centuries.
Turkish television series have reached South America after capturing the film market of the Indian subcontinent, the Middle East, the Caucasus, Central Asia and the Balkans.

Relations between Saudi Arabia and its allies, particularly the United Arab Emirates, have deteriorated with Turkey in recent months due to suspicions of Ankara ’ s links to Muslim groups, including Egypt ’ s Muslim Brotherhood.
Turkey is gaining influence in Africa, with 15% of respondents naming it as a country with a good image.
Turkey's emphasis is on the education system, working with the new generation in Russia and other post-Soviet countries.
The media monitoring center at the Press and Information Agency of Uzbekistan concluded that the series contradicts national mentality and traditions, as well as family values of Uzbeks.

A closed-door meeting between Imran Khan, Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, and Malaysian Prime Minister Mahathir Mohamad, held behind closed doors at the UN General Assembly, may be the reason for Khan's support of the series.
The series has been dubbed the Islamic Game and Throne, a historical series of the Turkey-made Artoghrol uprising, aired on Pakistani state television since April and has gained a lot of fans.
Turkish expansion continues in Georgia.
MOXIE converts carbon dioxide into oxygen and carbon monoxide (CO)
At present, the amount of carbon dioxide on Mars is not enough to make it habitable for humans.
MOXIE is a device as small as a toaster
The Mars oxygen field resource utilization experimental instrument, MOXIE, makes 5 grams of oxygen from carbon dioxide in the Martian atmosphere.
The Perseverance rover has a device for obtaining oxygen from the Martian atmosphere.
Nitrogen-fixing, oxygen-producing cyanobacteria can be grown on Mars at low pressure under controlled conditions using exclusively local ingredients.
NASA plans to use the MOXIE system for a manned mission to Mars in the next 10 to 15 years.
Oxygen obtained from the Martian atmosphere can be used for breathing and as fuel for rockets.
The Luna 365 experiment is the longest and most closed biological regenerative life support system experiment in the world
The cyanobacteria layer produces water and oxygen for astronauts.
Oxygen reserves are replenished by the electrolysis of water on the International Space Station.

A test vehicle for extracting oxygen from the Martian atmosphere, as well as a "space helicopter" that is set to fly for the first time on a planet other than Earth, are among those on the Perseverance rover.
Cyanobacteria have retained their ability to grow in water containing only Martian dust, and can still be used to feed other microbes.
A reactor to produce oxygen for astronauts who will fly to the moon, Mars and other space objects, created by American scientists.
The rover will collect samples of Martian soil and place them in sterile containers.
Cyanobacteria are an invaluable source of oxygen.
This is the first time that humans have used instruments in outer space to convert other gases into oxygen.
The bacteria that release oxygen can grow freely in the soil and atmospheric conditions of Mars, simulated on Earth, and also be used as food for other life forms.
MOXIE works by separating oxygen atoms from carbon dioxide molecules.
Bayer was ordered to pay compensation in the amount of $2 billion.
Monsanto has been sued in the United States for suspected cancer caused by the herbicide Roundup, and Bayer is seeking to settle the lawsuit with $11 billion in damages.
Monsanto must pay $289 million to a man with terminal lymphoma

Bayer AG will announce that it has reached an approximately $10.5 billion agreement with plaintiffs in the United States who have accused its Roundup herbicide of causing cancer to settle tens of thousands of lawsuits.
More than 13,400 similar lawsuits against Bayer have been filed in the US courts
Since Bayer acquired Monsanto for US $63 billion, it has faced about 18,400 lawsuits in the United States.
Federal Judge Vince Chhabria said the settlement proposal was “clearly unreasonable ” for Roundup users who had not yet been diagnosed with cancer.
Austria bans glyphosate
Johnson was awarded $250 million in damages in the form of punishment of the manufacturer and $39 million in compensation for losses.
Bayer has been accused by farmers of causing cancer by the Roundup herbicide product.
Bayer’s stock price has fallen 42% since the acquisition was completed in June 2018.
A court in the United States halved compensation for cancer patients from the herbicide Roundup
Bayer, the German chemical company, said on Wednesday that a legal dispute over cancer caused by its Roundup brand herbicide has reached a $2 billion settlement with regulators.
There are more than 11,200 similar lawsuits involving herbicides in the United States.
The claimants have not yet formally agreed to accept this compensation
The UN agency classified glyphosate as “probably carcinogenic” in 2015
Monsanto Co. will pay $2 billion in punitive damages and $55 million in compensatory damages.
The herbicide Roundup is alleged to be a carcinogen in over 18,000 US lawsuits.

German chemical-pharmaceutical concern Bayer has offered to pay $8 billion to satisfy more than 18,000 US lawsuits.
The main ingredient of the herbicide is "glyphosate", which is not a new drug in Taiwan and was used in wheat, soybeans, beans, almonds, asparagus, pear fruits and other crops.
The painting is estimated at 60 million euros.
A gardener at the Ricci Oddi Modern Art Gallery in Piacenza, Italy, found a painting hiding in a plastic bag within an external wall while clearing away ivy in December.
The painting was stolen on February 22, 1997 from the Ricci Oddi Gallery of Contemporary Art as preparations for the Klimt exhibition were underway.
Upon the discovery of the painting, the thieves admitted that they had returned the artwork to the scene of the theft.

Italian prosecutor Ornella Chicca said the actual painting was verified as authentic, but it remains unclear whether the painting was later smuggled back into the wall mezzanine or was hidden there from the beginning, pending further examination and clarification.

Due to the closure of galleries and museums in Italy due to the spread of the coronavirus, it has not been possible for the public to visit the work in person, and officials of the Ricci Odi Gallery have provided the opportunity to visit the painting in its former exhibition.
The painting is one of the last works by Gustav Klimt, painted in 1916-1917.
The painting features a special symbolic pattern.
The painting was found in a cavity in the wall covered by an iron door, and inside lay a painting wrapped in a black plastic garbage bag.
In 1997, the police speculated that the prisoner had removed the frame from the roof of the gallery and hung the work, but the search was fruitless for many years.
The thieves said in a letter to Ermanno Marian, an employee of the newspaper Libertà, that they stole the work of the Austrian artist worth 60 million euros in February 1997, and then returned it.
The picture “Portrait of a Woman ” by the Austrian artist Gustav Klimt lay in the wall for 22 years.
A gardener was cleaning the ivy of the museum’s outer wall when he found a metal plate on the wall, and when he opened it, he found a canvas painting in a black sackcloth bag.

Two men admitted that 22 years ago they stole and then returned the painting by Gustav Klimt “Portrait of a Woman” and not all the time kept it in a niche in the wall of the Ricci Oddi gallery in Piacenza, Italy, where it was found by gardeners.
Police believe the thieves used a device such as a fishing rod to remove the painting from the wall.
Prosecutor Ornella Chicca confirmed that the painting is real using infrared radiation and other non-invasive detection methods.
Climate change will affect the areas in which most of the crop is now grown.

Climate change and rising temperatures are being witnessed along with drought.
The prediction of 14 million tons of wheat from 6 million hectares of land will not be realized due to reduced rainfall.

The cultivation pattern in the country should be revised to optimize water resources and maximize profits from agricultural activity.

The film "There Is No Evil", directed by Iranian dissident director Mohammad Rasoulof, won the Golden Bear Award for Best Picture at the Berlin Film Festival.
This film narrates the true story of a person named Hossein Sabzian who introduces himself as a well-known director.
“Deer” is one of the most prominent works made with a political theme in the entire history of Iranian cinema.
The Iranian film Innocence won the Golden Bear Award, the highest award at the festival.
The film "Offside" tells a story of the restrictions and exclusion of Iranian women.
Mohammad Rasoulof was sentenced by an Iranian court to one year in prison, and banned from going abroad and participating in social activities for two years.

The film explores our responsibilities and choices in life.

The film "The Devil Does Not Exist" was made in secret.
Mohammad Rasulof was forbidden to leave the country and engage in cinema since 2017.
Majidi is one of the most important and influential filmmakers of Iranian cinema.

Hatami Kia said "A film will be made and it will be shown that Iranians are liars and then this film will win an Oscar, why should I not protest and speak the truth, unless my policy is determined by the Oscar".
The ending of the film "Deer" was changed completely after its initial screening at the Tehran Film Festival.
Jafar Panahi's film "Offside" won several awards, including the Silver Bear of the Berlin Festival.
The film The Evil and Their Distance breaks the boundaries of national and cultural restrictions.
Mohammad Rasoulof was forbidden to travel outside the country for political reasons and could not personally receive the award.

At least four people were killed, including two children aged five and eight, and 15 others were rescued when a boat carrying human snakes sank off the British Channel off Dunkirk, France, on Tuesday.
Most of the illegal refugees in Calais are from Syria, Eritrea, Sudan and Ethiopia.
The victims may have been Iranian.
British and French Coast Guard vessels have picked up about 300 people trying to cross the English Channel in inflatable boats to get to the UK since November.
The French government will double the number of police patrols on its shores as part of an agreement with Britain to stop migrants from crossing the Channel

A British Home Office official revealed that authorities are considering a series of measures to stop illegal immigrants from sneaking into the UK by boat through the British Channel to apply for asylum
The English Channel is known for strong winds, high currents and heavy sea traffic, but many people are still smuggled from northern France to the United Kingdom.

Christophe Castaner announced via Twitter on Sunday that he had spoken by phone with Javid and that they had agreed to tighter monitoring of the English Channel route to counter illegal immigration to Britain.
Each migrant pays 3-4 thousand pounds for the crossing, according to former British Coast Guard officer Andy Roberts.
235 illegal migrants in 17 boats attempting to cross the English Channel from northern France were intercepted by British Coast Guard and Border Guard officers on Thursday.
The migrants were identified in a small inflatable boat about 1.5 kilometers off the coast of the city of Calais and rescued by border guards
Migrants who try to get into the kingdom on inflatable boats often go on a journey from safe countries of the European Union, where they could ask for asylum
The issue of migrants attempting to reach the UK via the English Channel on kayaks, inflatable boats and small vessels is nothing new, and dozens have been rescued and detained in recent months.
Home Office officials will meet with French officials this week to pressure the French side to stop illegal immigration in the British Channel.
The UK made changes to the immigration system to make it harder for people who enter the country illegally.

British Prime Minister Boris Johnson expressed sympathy for the families of the victims and stressed that the British government, along with the French authorities, will fight the smuggling gangs that prepare the dangerous journey of these vulnerable people.
France demands £30m from Britain to prevent more migrants from crossing the Channel.
British officials confirmed that the 17 migrants, including three children, were able to reach British shores by hijacking a 12-meter fishing boat near the French port of Calais early Tuesday.
Microsoft co-founder Paul Allen announced the Stratolaunch project in 2011 to develop a giant aircraft that can replace rockets to send an artificial satellite spacecraft into low Earth orbit.
Stratolaunch is the world's largest aircraft with a total wingspan of 117 meters.
The test of the Stratolaunch aircraft passed successfully
The plane took off from a test site in the Mojave Desert, the flight lasted 2.5 hours at an altitude of more than 5 kilometers with a maximum speed of 304 kilometers per hour.
The Stratolaunch is propelled by six 747 engines with a payload of nearly 230 tons.

The aircraft is expected to be an airborne launch platform to put small satellites into orbit, with the goal of providing the military, private companies and even NASA with a more affordable way to enter space.
Stratolaunch plans to carry future spacecraft to high altitude.
During its test flight on Thursday, Stratolaunch reached an altitude of 14,000 feet (4,267 m) and a top speed of 199 mph (320 km / h).
The Stratolaunch made its first flight in April 2019.
The Stratolaunch aircraft is equipped with six twin-circuit Pratt & Whitney turbojet engines
The carrier aircraft of the Stratolaunch project is designed to launch rockets into orbit from an altitude of 9.1 thousand meters.
Footage of the flight of the new aircraft Stratolaunch Systems appeared in the network.
Stratolaunch Systems main sponsor is Paul Allen
The Stratolaunch aircraft has two fuselages and a wingspan of 117 meters, which is more than the wingspan of any other aircraft that has ever taken to the sky.
The world’s largest aircraft took off for the first time.
Stratolaunch expects another test flight in 2019.
The Stratolaunch aircraft has a maximum take-off weight of 589.7 tons.
The aircraft can carry a 110-ton rocket from the ground to 35,000 feet in the stratosphere, and then fire the rocket, launch it out of the atmosphere, and then send satellites into low Earth orbit.
A video of tests of the prototype aircraft Stratolaunch appeared on the Network.
The fan holding the sign seemed to be looking into the cameras, not at the approaching riders.
One cyclist had to be removed from the race because of the injuries
Tour de France spokesman Pierre Yves told AFP that charges will be brought against the woman
A fan caused a huge crash at the Tour de France that took down a large group of riders in the first stage.
A video shows the driver, Mikhail Vasiliev, shouting to the cyclist, “The road was built for whom?! ” after the ram attempt.
The charge is "deliberate violation of safety regulations and causing injuries that could prevent someone from working for up to three months."
The accident was caused by a fan who went out on the track with a poster to attract the attention of television broadcasters.
Today, June 30, the woman was identified and detained in the city of Landerno.
One rider had to leave the tour completely, and eight riders were injured as a result of the accident.
The audience was also injured in the accident.
The fan pushed the Russian cyclist Maxim Belkov from the bike at the competition “Vuelta España ”.
The court found that Mikhail Vasiliev deliberately maneuvered out of the roadway, forcing the cyclist to change trajectory.
French gendarmerie lieutenant colonel Joël Scherer said the woman could face charges of clearly and deliberately violating the duty of care and accidentally causing short-term harm.
The accident caused a cyclist named Josha Sutherlin of the DSM to be suspended from the race.
The woman turned her back on the driver and raised a sign reading “Greet Grandpa and Grandma ” in front of the camera.
Several cyclists were injured, 21 of which required medical attention
The vice president of the Tour de France called for legal action against the woman, saying: “We will sue her for her very bad behavior.
The incident took place in the first leg of the Tour de France, between the cities of Britanny and Landerneau in the north-west.
The driver, Mikhail Vasiliev, pointed to a bicycle path passing near the roadway after the incident.
If the affected riders file lawsuits against her, the punishment may be more severe - up to a year in prison.
A woman with a poster tried to get into the frame in front of the peloton and was hit by a German cyclist Tony Martin.
The astronauts, who went to the International Space Station on Expedition 64 / 65, will spend about six months on the International Space Station and then return to Earth in May 2021.
The successful launch of Dragon could revolutionize the aerospace industry.
Crew Dragon can be used multiple times for space missions.
SpaceX participates in the NASA Commercial Crew Program to deliver astronauts into space.

American astronauts have been sent into orbit on Russian Soyuz spacecraft for a fee for the past 9 years
SpaceX plans to carry private company astronauts and even tourists to space.

SpaceX launched a manned spacecraft Crew Dragon to the ISS for the first time.

Four survivors of the victims of Flight 752 have publicly filed a lawsuit against Iran in Ontario, along with a woman who does not want to be named.

The Court of Appeals dismissed the arguments of Iranian government lawyers, accusing the Islamic Republic of seeking to undermine the Justice for Victims of Terrorism Act passed by the Canadian Parliament.
Iran's actions demanded that the victims' families be compensated hundreds of millions of Canadian dollars.
The Supreme Court of the Canadian province of Ontario ruled that Iran committed a terrorist attack by shooting down Boeing 737 of Ukraine International Airlines.

Japan has decided to withdraw from the International Whaling Commission (IWC).
Whale meat is the main source of protein for poor Japanese society after World War II, but consumption has declined significantly in recent decades.
Scientists have confirmed a sufficient number of whales
Commercial whaling has resumed in Japan after 31 years
Japan begins third year of commercial whaling
In the 1960s, the Japanese consumed 200,000 metric tons of whale meat a year, but in recent years it has been greatly reduced, with annual consumption of only about 5,000 metric tons.
Japanese officials continue to hunt whales on ships in the name of traditional customs and scientific research.
Japan’s whaling program has been widely condemned
Japan proposed to the IWC in September to restart commercial whaling, but the proposal was rejected by countries including Australia.
Eating whale meat is a national tradition in Japan
122 pregnant female whales were hunted by Japanese hunters
The decision of the Japanese authorities was criticized by activists and countries not engaged in whaling
Greenpeace Japan appealed to the Government of Japan to reconsider the decision to resume commercial whaling
Five whaling vessels from different regions gathered at Kushiro Port in Hokkaido to hunt minke whales, sei whales and booi whales on Monday.
Some whale species are still endangered, while the baleen whale, the main target of Japan's catch, is not endangered.
A ceremony was held this morning at the port of Kushiro, Hokkaido

Japan calls the consumption of whale meat part of the country’s culture

Hemingway uses the "iceberg" as a metaphor to interpret his own creative style.
In the 1930s, Najaf Daryabandari translated his first novel, A Farewell to Arms, by Ernest Hemingway.
